args.content:
M Murmur
Features Reliability GitHub

Ship agent code you can trust.
That's the murmur promise.

Murmur is an open-source reliability harness that catches flaky coding-agent outputs, measures pass@k variance, and validates every generation before it touches your codebase.

Star on GitHub How it works
94.3% pass@1 Single-pass accuracy across 500 agent runs on SWE-bench
99.7% pass^k (k=5) Consensus reliability with majority-vote sampling
±1.2% variance Output stability across temperature ranges 0.0–0.8

Three layers of reliability

Murmur sits between your agent and your repository, applying deterministic checks at every step.

⚖

Deterministic Guardrails

Define schemas, lint rules, and diff-size thresholds that block unreliable agent outputs before they open a pull request. No flaky generations reach your team.

☷

Variance Tracking

Every generation run is logged with pass@1, pass^k, and semantic-diff metrics. Detect drift in agent behavior across model updates, prompt changes, or temperature tweaks.

☰

Open Telemetry Export

Native OTLP exporter sends reliability signals straight into your existing Grafana, Datadog, or Honeycomb dashboards. No new tooling required.

What engineers are saying

We run thirty coding agents in parallel across a monorepo with two million lines of Rust. Before Murmur, one bad generation could cascade into hours of broken CI. Now our gate checks catch 97% of flaky outputs before they land. It's not optional — it's infrastructure.

Dr. Amara Osei Staff Engineer, Reliability Infrastructure — Noxus Systems
Murmur

Open-source reliability for the agent era.

GitHub Discord X RSS

© 2025 Murmur OSS. Apache 2.0 licensed.

murmur
Features Metrics Docs GitHub

Every agent needs a murmur.

Murmur is the open-source reliability harness that silently checks your coding agent’s work, ensuring every output is safe, correct, and production-ready.

Get Started View on GitHub

Built for trust at scale

Continuous Verification

Murmur evaluates every completion against a golden suite of tests. When your agent hallucinates a broken API or invents a parameter, Murmur catches it before it reaches your pipeline.

Drift Detection

Behavior drift is silent and deadly. Murmur tracks pass@1 and pass^k metrics over time, alerting you the moment your agent’s reliability deviates from baseline.

Open Source & Self‑Hosted

No black boxes. Murmur runs in your infrastructure, plugging into any agent that speaks JSON. All checks are transparent, auditable, and customizable.

Reliability in numbers

89.2% pass@1 single sample accuracy
94.7% pass^k k=10 consensus score
0.12 variance across random seeds

Murmur gives us the confidence to integrate AI‑generated code into our CI. It’s the silent partner every engineering team needs.

— Jordan Lee, Staff Engineer at Orbit Systems
murmur
GitHub Twitter Discord

© 2025 Murmur Project. All rights reserved.

murmur
Features Metrics About

Trust your agents with murmur

The open‑source reliability harness that keeps coding agents honest—every run, every commit.

Get started on GitHub
☑

Continuous Verification

Every agent edit is checked against your full test suite before it can land. No silent regressions, no broken builds.

⚙

Regression Guardrails

Define pass@k thresholds and variance limits. Merges that degrade reliability are automatically blocked.

📄

Transparent Auditing

Every run, every failure, every metric is captured in an open, tamper-proof log you can inspect at any time.

pass@1 94.7%
pass^k (k=5) 98.2%
variance 0.003

“Murmur gave us confidence to deploy agent‑written code daily. Our pass@1 jumped from 68% to 96% in three weeks.”

— Taylor, Engineering Lead at Finch

Open source under Apache 2.0 — © 2025 Murmur

GitHub Twitter Discord

murmur
Features Metrics About

Ship with murmur, fail with proof

The open‑source reliability harness that gives every coding agent an auditable reputation—before bad code reaches your team.

Explore on GitHub
☑

Continuous Verification

Every agent edit runs against your full test harness on every push. A failed run blocks the merge and notifies the team immediately.

☘

Regression Guardrails

Declare per-repo thresholds for pass@1 and pass^k. When reliability drops below your floor, merges lock automatically.

☷

Transparent Auditing

Every run, metric, and decision is recorded in an open, tamper‑proof log. Prove compliance or debug failures with a single query.

pass@1 94.7%
pass^k (k=5) 98.2%
variance 0.003

“Murmur gave us confidence to ship agent‑written code every day. Our pass@1 rose from 68% to 96% in three weeks without slowing velocity.”

— Taylor, Engineering Lead at Finch

Open source under Apache 2.0 — © 2025 Murmur

GitHub Twitter Discord

◈ Murmur
  • Features
  • Metrics
  • Docs
  • GitHub

Open-source • v0.4.1 • Apache 2.0

Stop guessing. Murmur your agent's reliability into certainty.

Murmur is a lightweight reliability harness that runs coding agents through repeatable, statistically rigorous benchmarks. Catch regressions before they ship, track pass@k drift across model updates, and publish verifiable reliability scores your team can trust.

Get started — it's open source Read the docs 
# murmur run ./benchmarks/humaneval
$ murmur eval --model=gpt-4o --samples=128
⏳ Running 128 samples per task…
✓ pass@1:  0.874  (±0.029)
✓ pass@5:  0.961  (±0.017)
! variance delta: +0.031 vs baseline
Report saved to ./reliability/murmur-2025-06-15.json

Live reliability snapshot

Every Murmur benchmark produces a standardized scorecard. Here's what an agent report looks like after 512 trials.

pass@1 0.847 Single-shot accuracy across 512 independent evaluations. The probability your agent solves the task correctly on the first attempt — no retries, no cherry-picking.
pass^k 0.968 Coverage metric: the fraction of tasks solved at least once when the agent is allowed k attempts per problem. Captures latent capability that pass@1 misses.
variance ±0.022 Standard deviation of the pass@1 estimator computed via bootstrap resampling. Narrower bands mean higher confidence in your reliability claims.

Why teams wire Murmur into CI

Three capabilities that turn agent reliability from a gut feeling into a measurable property.

⏻

Deterministic replay

Every Murmur run seeds the environment, pins dependency versions, and logs every prompt–completion pair. A failing test today will fail identically six months from now — making regression bisection trivial and audits reproducible.

⧉

Multi-model diffing

Run identical task suites across different models, temperatures, or system prompts and Murmur emits a ranked diff. Spot capability tradeoffs instantly: did that prompt tweak raise pass@1 but silently degrade pass@10?

⚠

Threshold gating

Set minimum reliability thresholds in a murmur.toml file and Murmur exits non-zero when scores dip. Drop it into GitHub Actions or a pre-commit hook and block merges that would silently erode agent quality.

"Before Murmur, we'd ship a new system prompt and pray. Now our CI catches a 3% pass@1 drop before it reaches a pull request. It's the difference between trusting vibes and trusting data."

Sasha Keene Staff Engineer, Tooling Infrastructure — Nines Optics

Murmur is free, open-source, and takes five minutes to wire up.

pip install murmur-harness. Drop a config file. Run your first reliability report today.

Star on GitHub — v0.4.1
◈ Murmur

Reliability harness for coding agents. Apache 2.0 licensed.

MURMUR
GitHub Twitter Discord

Murmur: Harness reliability for your coding agents

The open‑source harness that turns brittle agent outputs into production‑grade determinism.

Explore on GitHub

Continuous Validation

Murmur automatically subjects your agent to a curated suite of benchmarks on every commit, catching regressions before they reach users.

Variance Analysis

Track pass@k consistency across multiple runs. Surface flaky prompts and eliminate nondeterministic behavior before it erodes trust.

Open Telemetry

Export reliability metrics to any observability stack. Murmur integrates with Prometheus, Datadog, and custom dashboards out of the box.

94.3% pass@1
88.7% pass^k
0.0012 Variance

Murmur gave us the confidence to deploy coding agents in production. The variance tracking alone saved us weeks of debugging.

— Dr. Ada Voss, AI Reliability Researcher at SynthLabs

© 2025 Murmur Project. Open‑source under MIT license.

Murmur
Features Metrics Story Star on GitHub
Open-source harness · v0.4.1

Every commit, every branch, murmur has your back

A reliability harness that wraps your coding agent and surfaces pass@k scores, drift alerts, and flake detection — directly in your CI logs.

Get started free Explore features

Harness Benchmarks — Live

pass@1 94.7%

First-shot accuracy across 8,400 held-out tasks from SWE-bench and HumanEval.

passk 98.3%

Best-of-8 consensus with majority voting and verifier re-ranks.

variance ±1.8%

Week-over-week score stability. Runs every 6 hours against the public harness.

Scores calculated from murmur-7b-instruct on 2025-07-10. Reproduced on identical seeds.

Three signals. Zero flaky deploys.

Murmur wraps your agent in a lightweight evaluation loop that speaks your CI's language.

Deterministic evals, every time

Murmur snapshots your agent's environment and replays the same 2,100 prompts on every run. No hidden state, no randomized seeds — just reproducible pass/fail signals you can graph over months.

Stress-tested concurrency

Spin up 64 parallel harness instances against your endpoint. Track tail latency, throughput degradation, and failure correlation so a 99th-percentile timeout never reaches production.

Drift detection built in

When a model vendor pushes a silent update or your prompt template shifts, Murmur's statistical checks raise a flag before your users notice the degradation.

Murmur

Open-source reliability harness for coding agents. Apache 2.0.

murmur
Features Metrics GitHub

murmur

The open‑source reliability harness for coding agents.

Ship AI‑assisted code with confidence. Evaluate every commit, detect drift the moment it appears, and keep your agents accountable — without surrendering visibility to a black box.

View on GitHub See the numbers

Live Reliability Benchmarks

pass@1 94.7% single‑sample accuracy
pass^k 99.3% k=10 consensus pass
variance ±1.2% week‑over‑week drift

What murmur does

▸

Continuous Evaluation

Every pull request triggers a full reliability sweep. Murmur runs your agent against a curated benchmark and surfaces pass@1, pass@k, and latency metrics before code reaches production — so you know exactly whether the latest prompt tweak helped or hurt.

●

Drift Detection

Prompts evolve and models shift beneath you — often silently. Murmur tracks variance across versions and alerts your team the moment reliability strays outside your thresholds. Catch the dip before your customers do.

■

Open Observability

No proprietary dashboards, no vendor lock‑in. Murmur exports every run as structured JSON logs and plugs into your existing stack — Prometheus, Grafana, or plain CSV. Your data stays yours, always.

Trusted by teams shipping agent‑generated code

Before Murmur, we were flying blind. Our coding agent’s pass@1 dropped from 91% to 76% over a month and we didn’t notice until a customer filed a bug. Now we catch drift within hours. It’s not optional — it’s infrastructure.

Sarah Chen Engineering Lead, Cascade Labs
murmur

Open‑source reliability for the agent era.

Product Features Benchmarks Documentation
Community GitHub Twitter / X Discord

© 2025 Murmur Contributors. Apache 2.0 licensed.

GH TW DC

Murmur — the open-source reliability harness for coding agents.

Measure, monitor, and improve the reliability of your AI‑assisted code. Stop guessing and start proving.

Star on GitHub →

Pass@1 Benchmarking

Quantify agent performance with rigorous pass@1 metrics. Murmur runs your agents through controlled scenarios to give you reproducible results.

Passᵏ Variance Analysis

Go beyond point estimates. Murmur computes passᵏ across multiple runs to expose variance and build confidence intervals.

Open‑Source & Extensible

Built for developers. Murmur is fully open‑source under the MIT license. Add your own harness, environments, and metrics.

84.7% pass@1
79.2% passᵏ
0.023 variance

“Murmur transformed how we evaluate our coding agents. We’ve reduced regression failures by 40% and gained confidence in every release.”

— Dr. Ada Chen, AI Platform Lead, Synthwave Labs

Murmur is an open‑source project.

GitHub Twitter Discord

Measure, monitor, and improve the reliability of your AI‑assisted code. Stop guessing whether your agents work. Start proving it with Murmur.

Star on GitHub →

Key Capabilities

◎

Pass@1 Benchmarking

Quantify agent performance with rigorous pass@1 metrics. Murmur runs your coding agents through controlled, reproducible scenarios so you can track real accuracy over time.

▤

Passᵈ Variance Analysis

Go beyond point estimates. Murmur computes passᵈ across multiple independent runs to expose variance, surface flaky behavior, and build robust confidence intervals.

★

Open‑Source & Extensible

Built for developers, by developers. Murmur is fully open‑source under the MIT license. Add custom harnesses, environments, and metrics without friction.

84.7% pass@1
79.2% passᵈ
0.023 variance

“Murmur transformed how we evaluate our coding agents. We’ve reduced regression failures by 40% and gained the confidence to ship agent‑generated code on every release.”

— Dr. Ada Chen, AI Platform Lead, Synthwave Labs

Open‑source reliability for autonomous code.

args.content:
Skip to main content
◆ Murmur
  • Features
  • Metrics
  • Why Murmur
  • GitHub

Ship code you can murmur about

Murmur is the open‑source reliability harness that stress-tests coding agents across thousands of real-world prompts. Catch regressions, track pass rates, and deploy agent-generated code with quiet confidence.

Star on GitHub See the numbers
// murmur harness run — 2025-01-17 const suite = harness.load("swe-bench"); const result = await suite.evaluate(agent); if (result.passRate < 0.92) throw result;

Reliability, measured

Murmur runs every agent through a gauntlet of 14,200+ curated prompts across 8 benchmarks — every commit, every release.

pass@1
94.7%

First-shot accuracy across SWE-bench Verified. No retries, no cherry-picking — one prompt, one answer, measured cold.

passk
98.3%

Coverage with k‑sample consensus. Murmur runs up to k‑16 samples and requires a supermajority before accepting the output.

variance
σ 2.1%

Standard deviation across 30 repeated runs. Low variance means your agent doesn't surprise you on Monday morning.

What Murmur gives you

A single harness that plugs into any agent runtime. No vendor lock-in, no proprietary dashboards — just open infrastructure for agent reliability.

Deterministic regression suites

Murmur captures every prompt-response pair into versioned suites. When your agent model updates, re-run the suite and get a single diff of what broke. No more guessing whether the new checkpoint is actually better.

Continuous reliability monitoring

Hook Murmur into CI and get a reliability score on every PR. Set thresholds for pass@1, pass^k consensus, and latency percentiles. If your agent regresses below the bar, the build goes red — automatically.

Community benchmarks, shared transparently

Every Murmur benchmark suite is a plain YAML file you can fork, share, and audit. The community maintains canonical suites for SWE-bench, HumanEval, and BashBench. Contribute your own and raise the bar for everyone.

What engineers are saying

Murmur

Open-source reliability for coding agents. Apache 2.0 licensed.

args.content:
Murmur
Features Metrics Testimonial

Murmur: The Open‑Source Reliability Harness for Coding Agents

Debug less, ship more — gain deep visibility into every prompt, tool call, and output.

Get Started on GitHub

Why Murmur?

◈

Reproducible Benchmarks

Run deterministic test suites across LLM providers and environments. Track pass@k consistency over time to catch regressions early.

⚡

Runtime Guardrails

Catch prompt leaks, token waste, and unsafe tool calls before they reach production. Murmur acts as a silent safety net for every invocation.

⎈

Collaborative Insights

Compare agent configurations side-by-side. Share results with your team via dashboards or CI, and build institutional knowledge around agent behaviour.

Measurable Trust

Pass@1 94.7%
Pass^k 89.2%
Variance 0.0032

Metrics from our latest benchmark suite across 10,000 tasks.

“Murmur gave our research team the confidence to ship updates weekly. We cut regression incidents by 70% and finally understand how prompt changes affect real‑world behaviour.”

— Dr. Elena Voss, AI Platform Lead
Murmur
G T D

© 2025 Murmur Project. Open‑source under Apache 2.0.
